steps.title: Classify equations by number of solutions
steps.content:
An equation's number of solutions depends on what happens when we simplify:
  • Contradiction (e.g., $5 = 0$): 0 solutions
  • Identity (e.g., $0 = 0$): infinite solutions
  • Conditional equation: exactly 1 solution

steps.title: Eliminate choices A, C, and D
steps.content:
Choice A: $\frac{1}{44} + x = x$ → Subtract $x$: $\frac{1}{44} = 0$ (contradiction, 0 solutions)

Choice C: $\frac{1}{44} - x = \frac{1}{44} - x$ → Same on both sides (identity, infinite solutions)

Choice D: $\frac{1}{44} - x = -x$ → Add $x$: $\frac{1}{44} = 0$ (contradiction, 0 solutions)

steps.title: Solve choice B
steps.content:
$$\frac{1}{44} + x = -x - \frac{1}{44}$$
Add $x$ to both sides:
$$\frac{1}{44} + 2x = -\frac{1}{44}$$
Subtract $\frac{1}{44}$:
$$2x = -\frac{2}{44}$$
Divide by 2:
$$x = -\frac{1}{44}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
